verses.2: “Hear my words, you wise men, And listen to me, you who understand.
verses.3: “For the ear tests words As the palate tastes food.
verses.4: “Let us choose for ourselves what is right; Let us understand among ourselves what is good.
verses.5: “For Job has said, ‘I am righteous, But God has taken away my right;
verses.6: Should I lie about my right? My wound is incurable, though I am without wrongdoing.’
verses.7: “What man is like Job, Who drinks up derision like water,
verses.8: Who goes in company with the workers of injustice, And walks with wicked people?
verses.9: “For he has said, ‘It is of no use to a man When he becomes friends with God.’
verses.10: “Therefore, listen to me, you men of understanding. Far be it from God to do evil, And from the Almighty to do wrong.
verses.11: “For He repays a person for his work, And lets things happen in correspondence to a man’s behavior.
verses.12: “God certainly will not act wickedly, And the Almighty will not pervert justice.
verses.13: “Who gave Him authority over the earth? And who has placed the whole world on Him?
verses.14: “If He were to determine to do so, If He were to gather His spirit and His breath to Himself,
verses.15: Humanity would perish together, And mankind would return to dust.
verses.16: “But if you have understanding, hear this; Listen to the sound of my words.
verses.17: “Shall one who hates justice rule? And will you condemn the righteous mighty One,
verses.18: Who says to a king, ‘You worthless one,’ To nobles, ‘You wicked one’;
verses.19: Who shows no partiality to the prominent, Nor regards the rich as above the poor, Since they are all the work of His hands?
verses.20: “In a moment they die, and at midnight People are shaken and pass away, And the powerful are taken away without a hand.
verses.21: “For His eyes are upon the ways of a person, And He sees all his steps.
verses.22: “There is no darkness or deep shadow Where the workers of injustice can hide themselves.
verses.23: “For He does not need to consider a person further, That he should go before God in judgment.
verses.24: “He breaks in pieces the mighty without investigation, And sets others in their place.
verses.25: “Therefore He knows their deeds, And He overthrows them in the night, And they are crushed.
verses.26: “He strikes them like the wicked In a public place,
verses.27: Because they turned aside from following Him, And had no regard for any of His ways,
verses.28: So that they caused the cry of the poor to come to Him, And that He would hear the cry of the afflicted—
verses.29: When He keeps quiet, who can condemn? And when He hides His face, who then can look at Him, That is, regarding both nation and a person?—
verses.30: “So that godless people would not rule, Nor be snares for the people.
verses.31: “For has anyone said to God, ‘I have endured punishment; I will not offend anymore;
verses.32: Teach me what I do not see; If I have done wrong, I will not do it again’?
verses.33: “Shall God repay on your terms, because you have rejected His? For you must choose, and not I; Therefore declare what you know.
verses.34: “Men of understanding will say to me, And a wise man who hears me,
verses.35: ‘Job speaks without knowledge, And his words are without wisdom.
verses.36: ‘Oh that Job were tested to the limit, Because he answers like sinners.
verses.37: ‘For he adds rebellion to his sin; He claps his hands among us, And multiplies his words against God.’”
